narrative: OMX30 faces headwinds from a sharp global tech selloff and risk-off sentiment despite maintaining a structural bull market position (+5.9% above 200-MA). The NASDAQ's 1.47% decline and AI trade reversal signal weakness in growth sectors, while rising US yields (+53bp) apply DCF compression to Nordic tech and real estate. Swedish exporters find partial support from a stronger USD/SEK (+0.60%), but the elevated VIX (17.89) and negative correlation signal (-0.950%) suggest caution. MACD below signal line and moderate ADX (22.2 with bearish DI bias) reduce conviction; only +0.6% above 50-MA offers thin buffer. Watch for US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET as a potential catalyst.
morningBrief: OMX30 opens into a risk-off environment with global equities under pressure from Asia weakness (Nikkei -4.03%, Shanghai -3.05%) and a NASDAQ selloff (-1.47%) on AI trade reversal. US 10Y yields surge 53bp to 4.569%, creating a bearish mix for Nordic tech and real estate exposure while favoring bank NIM expansion. Key support at 3085 (lower Bollinger band) and pivot watch at 3123; resistance remains 3180 (50-MA, +0.6% overhead). Exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson) gain modest tailwind from USD/SEK strength (+0.60%), but MACD below signal and ADX 22.2 (moderate, bearish DI bias) cut confidence. Play defensive rotation into AstraZeneca and Essity; avoid cyclical leverage into Industrials. US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) could trigger sharp intraday volatility.
drivers: Broad global selloff led by Asia (Nikkei -4.03%, Shanghai -3.05%) and NASDAQ -1.47%
US Treasury yields spike +53bp to 4.569% pressuring tech valuations and real estate
VIX jump +6.93% to 17.89 signals elevated risk aversion despite bull regime
Strong USD/SEK (+0.60%) supports exporters but offset by tech sector weakness
OMX30 remains +5.9% above 200-MA but RSI neutral (48.22) and MACD below signal

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +53bp to 4.569% widens net interest margin expectations despite risk-off backdrop.
NASDAQ -1.47% and AI trade reversal create sentiment headwind; rising yields compress DCF multiples.
US 10Y +53bp increases refinancing costs and cap rate expansion risk; risk-off sentiment pressures valuations.
USD/SEK +0.60% supports export competitiveness, offsetting modest input cost pressure from oil +0.52%.
Gold +0.37% to 4000 and stable copper (flat) provide revenue support; risk-off boosts gold bid.
Risk-off sentiment drives defensive rotation; stable fundamentals in lower-growth, recession-resistant names.

watchList.reason: Defensive rotation into pharma on elevated VIX (17.89) and risk-off tone; recession-resistant earnings.
NASDAQ weakness (-1.47%) and tech sentiment headwind override USD/SEK tailwind; wait for MACD recovery.
Yield curve steepening (+53bp) unlocks NIM expansion; trading near 50-MA support; cyclical upside on rate normalization.
Gold +0.37% to 4000 supports mining economics; risk-off backdrop favors safe-haven bid; watch copper for downside.
USD/SEK +0.60% supports export pricing but industrial cycle remains soft; wait for demand signals post-US data.

econCalendarAlert: No material Swedish economic releases today. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is key catalyst; expectations ~232K. Watch for any surprise that could impact USD/SEK and risk sentiment into close.
topRisk: Escalation in US-China trade tensions or further AI trade reversal could spike VIX above 20, forcing liquidation across all Nordic risk assets. Real estate and tech sectors most exposed. Secondary risk: US Jobless Claims miss could trigger rapid yield repricing and intraday volatility.
technicalSummary: Bull regime intact: +5.9% above 200-MA, but momentum fading (+0.6% above 50-MA only)
MACD below signal line (hist=-7.770) — bearish divergence in uptrend, potential mean reversion
RSI 48.22 (neutral) — no overbought risk, but lack of conviction on strength
ADX 22.2 (moderate, bearish DI bias) — reduces confidence per rule; apply 30% haircut to magnitude
Bollinger %B=0.31 (lower half) — price pressing lower band; 3085 support is critical
52W position at 88% of range, -2.8% from high — limited downside cushion in risk-off scenario
